compiled_state.title: RED: Write failing tests for check-portability.sh
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Description

Create tests/scripts/test-check-portability.sh with behavioral tests for the portability lint script. These tests verify that check-portability.sh correctly detects hardcoded home-directory paths and respects the portability-ok suppression.

### Tests to add:

1. test_detects_macos_hardcoded_path — Create temp file with /Users/testuser/some/path, pass to check-portability.sh, assert exit non-zero. Test approach: create fixture, run script, assert exit code.

2. test_detects_linux_hardcoded_path — Create temp file with /home/testuser/some/path, assert exit non-zero. Test approach: same as above with Linux pattern.

3. test_passes_clean_file — Create temp file with no hardcoded paths (only relative paths, /usr/bin, /etc), assert exit 0. Test approach: create clean fixture, run script, assert exit 0.

4. test_portability_ok_suppression — Create temp file with /Users/testuser/path # portability-ok, assert exit 0 (suppressed line not flagged). Test approach: create fixture with suppression comment, run script, assert line is excluded from matches.

5. test_multiple_files_mixed — Pass multiple files (one clean, one dirty), assert exit non-zero and only dirty file is reported. Test approach: create two fixtures, run script on both, assert correct file reported.

6. test_script_does_not_flag_own_regex — The script itself contains path patterns in its grep regex. Verify running the script on itself does not produce a false positive (the regex line has # portability-ok). Test approach: run check-portability.sh on its own source, assert exit 0.

Add .test-index RED markers. Fuzzy match: checkportabilitysh is substring of testcheckportabilitysh — no .test-index entry needed beyond RED markers.

### File Impact
- tests/scripts/test-check-portability.sh (create)
- .test-index (modify: add RED markers)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] bash tests/run-all.sh passes (exit 0) — RED tests tolerated via .test-index markers
  Verify: bash tests/run-all.sh
- [ ] Six test functions exist in test-check-portability.sh
  Verify: grep -cE '^test_' tests/scripts/test-check-portability.sh | awk '{exit ($1 < 6)}'
- [ ] .test-index RED markers exist for portability tests
  Verify: grep -q 'test-check-portability.sh \[test_detects_macos_hardcoded_path\]' .test-index
- [ ] Tests create fixture files with hardcoded paths (behavioral, not structural)
  Verify: grep -q 'mktemp\|TMPDIR\|tmp_file' tests/scripts/test-check-portability.sh

comments.body: Fixed: 6 RED tests for check-portability.sh written (6 fail — script not yet created)
comments.author: Test
